scenario: US blockade in effect but vessel-scoped to Iranian ports (non-Iranian tankers transit freely, LSEG/Kpler Apr 14); Trump signals second Islamabad round within 'two days'; Brent retraces to ~$93 (Apr 14 $94.79 CNBC, Apr 15 WTI $90.92 TradingEconomics); tail risk 32%; Apr 21 ceasefire expiry is binary catalyst

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases on Brent retrace and fresh-talks signal. Blockade vessel-scoping confirmed Apr 14 means non-Iranian fertilizer flows can transit Hormuz. Gulf urea dislocation persists pending formal Hormuz normalization. Source: Barchart ZWK26.

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel demand correlation eases further as Brent retraces to ~$93 on fresh Islamabad talks signal. Indonesian ethanol demand trajectory softens. Fertilizer and freight costs remain elevated but directional easing aligns with broader risk-premium unwind.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker cost relief as Brent eases to ~$93 reduces freight-driven soy pressure. Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins improve. Blockade vessel-scoping means non-Iranian cargo flows persist; structural spreads above pre-crisis remain but compressing.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO softens as Brent crude correlation reverses on fresh-talks signal. Biodiesel feedstock premium compresses with oil at ~$93/bbl. Indonesia's B40 mandate remains intact. Fertilizer disruption for next yield cycle is easing but not resolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure eases as freight and energy input costs fall with Brent retrace. Non-Iranian tanker transit via Hormuz (3 vessels Apr 14) supports fertilizer delivery. Q2 planting season fertilizer availability remains the structural constraint.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha tracks Brent lower on fresh-talks signal. Blockade vessel-scoping (LSEG/Kpler Apr 14) preserves non-Iranian Gulf condensate flow. Qatar force majeure unresolved - full Ras Laffan restart unlikely before late Aug 2026 (The National). Jurong Island and Map Ta Phut feedstock pressure easing.

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene follows naphtha lower on fresh-talks signal. SABIC and QAPCO export bottlenecks ease as non-Iranian tankers transit Hormuz unimpeded. Agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times improving. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eases on fresh-talks signal. US to let Iran oil sanctions waiver expire (Bloomberg Apr 14) tightens enforcement on Iranian methanol exports specifically. Qatar force majeure unresolved. China MTO complexes adjust to lower feedstock trajectory. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases on fresh-talks signal but remains structurally elevated: Qafco force majeure still in effect, Ras Laffan full restart unlikely before late Aug 2026. US waiver expiry tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Egypt and India continue buying at premium. Supply normalization requires formal Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases as fresh-talks signal reduces tail risk of Saudi export dislocation. Saudi Ma'aden shipping remains disrupted; US Gulf supply continues filling the gap. Wet-season planting pressure in Philippines and Vietnam persists through April.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs ease with fresh-talks signal as war-risk premiums partially unwind. Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP - freight normalization is the main transmission channel. Palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia benefit from lower input cost trajectory.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI eases on fresh-talks signal as Hormuz normalization hopes re-emerge. Non-Iranian tankers transiting Hormuz (3 vessels Apr 14, LSEG/Kpler) suggests Cape rerouting demand could reverse gradually. Full normalization still depends on P&I restoration post formal Hormuz reopening. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates ease as non-Iranian tanker transit confirmed (LSEG/Kpler Apr 14). US blockade scope narrower than feared; commercial Hormuz flows partially preserved. Full P&I restoration requires formal reopening + political settlement - Apr 21 ceasefire expiry is the key catalyst. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Container rates ease on fresh-talks signal. Asia-Europe rates remain above pre-crisis levels as Suez normalization requires P&I restoration and formal Hormuz reopening. Southeast Asian manufacturers see improved lead-time expectations. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (est.).
